title: Fix CLI exit code on parse errors
analysis:
The CLI's parse_and_compile() function (main.rs:31-60) prints parse errors to stderr but always returns Ok(compiled), meaning 'reify check' and 'reify build' exit 0 even when the source has parse errors. Additionally, cmd_check() only checks constraint satisfaction (not compiler diagnostics) and cmd_build() only checks geometry output presence (not compiler diagnostics), so files with compiler ERROR-level diagnostics (unresolved names, type mismatches, etc.) can also incorrectly exit 0. cmd_lsp() uses .expect() for Tokio runtime creation instead of proper error handling.

The fix has three parts: (1) parse_and_compile() returns Err(ExitCode::FAILURE) when parsed.errors is non-empty, (2) cmd_check() and cmd_build() check compiled.diagnostics for Severity::Error and return FAILURE before proceeding to evaluation/build, (3) cmd_lsp() replaces .expect() with match + proper error return.

The compiler (reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:583-588) forwards parse errors as Diagnostic::warning, not error. This is intentional — the CLI gates on parse errors directly in parse_and_compile(). The Severity::Error diagnostic check in cmd_check/cmd_build catches semantic compiler errors that parse successfully but fail compilation (unresolved names on line 397, unknown units on line 376, dimension mismatches on line 425, etc.).

Existing tests use Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_reify")) to spawn the binary and assert on exit status and stdout/stderr. Two new fixture files are needed: one with parse errors, one with compiler errors. A new cli_build.rs test file is needed for build-specific integration tests (none exist currently beyond the smoke test).

prerequisites.description: Create fixture file crates/reify-cli/tests/fixtures/bracket_parse_error.ri with invalid syntax that causes parse errors. Use a structure with broken syntax like garbled tokens inside the body, e.g. 'structure Bracket { @@@ not valid }'. The parser (Tree-sitter based, error-recovering) will produce a ParsedModule with non-empty .errors vec.

Create fixture file crates/reify-cli/tests/fixtures/bracket_compile_error.ri with valid syntax but semantic compiler errors. Use a structure that references an unresolved name, e.g. 'structure Bracket { let volume = unknown_name }'. This triggers Diagnostic::error("unresolved name: unknown_name") at compiler lib.rs:396-397.

steps.description: Write failing test check_parse_error_exits_failure in cli_check.rs: spawn 'reify check bracket_parse_error.ri', assert !output.status.success() and stderr contains 'Parse error'. This WILL FAIL because parse_and_compile() returns Ok(compiled) even with parse errors, so check proceeds to constraint evaluation and exits 0.

Fix parse_and_compile() in main.rs: inside the existing 'if !parsed.errors.is_empty()' block (lines 47-51), add 'return Err(ExitCode::FAILURE);' after the error-printing loop. This makes the function return Err before compilation when parse errors are present. Step-1 test should now pass.

Write test build_parse_error_exits_failure in a new cli_build.rs test file: spawn 'reify build bracket_parse_error.ri -o /tmp/reify_test_out.step', assert !output.status.success() and stderr contains 'Parse error'. Include a fixture_path() helper matching the pattern in cli_check.rs. This should PASS immediately since parse_and_compile's Err return is shared by both cmd_check and cmd_build.

Write failing test check_compile_error_exits_failure in cli_check.rs: spawn 'reify check bracket_compile_error.ri', assert !output.status.success() and stderr contains 'error:' (the Severity::Error display format). This WILL FAIL because cmd_check() ignores compiled.diagnostics and only checks constraint satisfaction — a file with compiler errors but no constraints would exit 0.

Fix cmd_check() in main.rs: after the parse_and_compile() Ok path (line 69), add a check: if compiled.diagnostics.iter().any(|d| d.severity == Severity::Error) then return ExitCode::FAILURE. Add 'Severity' to the reify_types use statement on line 6. The diagnostics are already printed by parse_and_compile, so no additional printing needed. Step-4 test should now pass.

Write failing test build_compile_error_exits_failure in cli_build.rs: spawn 'reify build bracket_compile_error.ri -o /tmp/reify_test_out.step', assert !output.status.success() and stderr contains 'error:'. This WILL FAIL because cmd_build() doesn't check compiler diagnostics — it relies solely on geometry_output being None, which may or may not happen depending on how far evaluation gets.

Fix cmd_build() in main.rs: after the parse_and_compile() Ok path (line 130), add the same Severity::Error diagnostic check as cmd_check. If any ERROR diagnostics exist, return ExitCode::FAILURE. Step-6 test should now pass.

Fix cmd_lsp() in main.rs: replace line 174 'tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().expect("failed to create tokio runtime")' with a match expression: match tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() { Ok(rt) => { rt.block_on(...); ExitCode::SUCCESS }, Err(e) => { eprintln!("Failed to create async runtime: {}", e); ExitCode::FAILURE } }. Defensive fix — no test needed since Tokio runtime failure is extremely hard to trigger in CI.

Run the full existing test suite for reify-cli to verify no regressions. Specifically: check_valid_bracket_exits_success (valid file still exits 0), check_violating_bracket_exits_failure (constraint violations still detected), check_nonexistent_file_exits_failure (file I/O errors still handled), and all smoke tests. All must pass.

design_decisions.decision: Return Err early from parse_and_compile on parse errors rather than changing compiler to forward them as Severity::Error
design_decisions.rationale: The compiler intentionally forwards parse errors as warnings (line 586: Diagnostic::warning) because it still attempts best-effort compilation from the partial AST for IDE/LSP use. Changing that severity would alter compiler semantics for all consumers. The CLI should gate on parse errors directly since proceeding to evaluation with a broken parse is never useful in CLI/CI context.

design_decisions.decision: Check diagnostics for Severity::Error AFTER parse_and_compile but BEFORE constraint evaluation / geometry build
design_decisions.rationale: If the compiler found semantic errors (unresolved names, type mismatches), running constraint evaluation or geometry build is wasteful and may produce confusing output. Early exit gives a clear signal to CI scripts. The diagnostics are already printed to stderr by parse_and_compile, so the check just scans and returns FAILURE.

design_decisions.decision: Create a new cli_build.rs test file for build command integration tests
design_decisions.rationale: No build-specific integration tests exist beyond the smoke test checking usage output. Following the existing pattern (cli_smoke.rs, cli_check.rs, cli_lsp.rs), build tests belong in their own file. The fixture_path helper is duplicated rather than extracted to a shared module, matching the existing cli_check.rs pattern.

design_decisions.decision: Use separate fixture files for parse errors vs compile errors rather than inline temp files
design_decisions.rationale: Existing tests use fixture files in tests/fixtures/. This is more readable, matches the project convention, and ensures the parser/compiler behavior is tested against stable input. Temp files add complexity (cleanup, path construction) for no benefit.

design_decisions.decision: No test for cmd_lsp Tokio runtime error handling
design_decisions.rationale: Tokio runtime creation only fails under extreme system conditions (e.g., exhausted file descriptors). Manufacturing this in a test is fragile and OS-dependent. The fix is a simple .expect() to match/Err pattern, which is verifiable by code review.

reuse.how: Define an identical fixture_path(name) helper in the new cli_build.rs, following the same env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") + "/tests/fixtures/" + name pattern.

All new tests follow the same pattern: Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_reify")).args([...]).stdin(Stdio::null()).stdout(Stdio::piped()).stderr(Stdio::piped()).output().expect(...), then assert on status and string content.

reuse.what: Severity enum with PartialEq derive and Display impl
reuse.how: Import Severity into main.rs via 'use reify_types::{..., Severity}' to check compiled.diagnostics entries. Severity derives PartialEq so d.severity == Severity::Error works directly. Display formats as 'error' matching the existing eprintln format string.

reuse.what: Existing bracket.ri fixture as template for error fixtures
reuse.how: bracket_parse_error.ri is a corrupted version of bracket.ri (broken syntax). bracket_compile_error.ri is a valid-syntax version that references an undefined name, triggering the compiler's unresolved-name error path (lib.rs:396).

The compile error fixture leverages the existing 'unresolved name' error path to produce a Diagnostic with Severity::Error, ensuring the cli diagnostic check is exercised against real compiler output.
